# CI constraints for the .venv-train install ONLY (pip `-c`). The requirements files are unpinned,
# but the train stack has a genuine version tension: stable-baselines3 (pulled by godot-rl) caps
# numpy<2.0, while ml_dtypes (pulled by onnxscript/onnxruntime) caps numpy>=2.1 on Python 3.13 —
# unsatisfiable. CI therefore builds .venv-train on Python 3.12, where ml_dtypes 0.5.4 needs only
# numpy>=1.26, so numpy 1.26.4 satisfies BOTH. These two pins make that resolution deterministic
# (both have cp312 Linux wheels, so nothing builds from source). The convert venv (.venv, py3.14)
# is installed WITHOUT these constraints (it has no sb3/ml_dtypes; numpy resolves to 2.x there).
numpy==1.26.4
ml_dtypes==0.5.4
